key_insight: LapidaryEngine enables fully conversational crystal design by using a pivot representation to translate between free-form text and crystal structures in both directions.
engineering_relevance: This could make materials discovery more interactive and user-driven, allowing engineers to iteratively refine candidates for stability, composition, and target properties from natural-language feedback.
method: Accepts free-form natural-language crystal requests instead of only structured inputs
Introduces a pivot representation for bidirectional text↔crystal translation without direct paired datasets
Supports iterative dialogue-style refinement and structural editing
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstrations across insulator discovery, stability optimization, compositional modification, and structural editing, indicating evaluation on multiple materials-design tasks.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the pivot representation and on how reliably the model interprets ambiguous user intent in real conversational settings.
